_comment: Kitsoki architecture & usage — ~20 slides for architects and engineers.

scenes.title: Kitsoki
scenes.subtitle: Architecture & usage for architects and principal engineers

scenes.body: A story is a deterministic directed cyclic graph with a scoped, typed context — the world.
scenes.lede: YAML is only the authoring surface. The runtime drives the graph. The LLM translates free text into one of the intents the current room exposes — it never decides what to do, never writes the world, never invents an action. Everything downstream of that translation is pure and replayable.
scenes.narration: YAML is the authoring surface. The runtime drives the graph. The LLM only translates — never decides.

scenes.title: Story anatomy
scenes.caption: A story is one YAML document. YAML is the authoring surface only — the runtime drives the graph, and the LLM is called for narrow sub-tasks alone.
scenes.narration: A story declares five concerns. The runtime drives the graph; the LLM is called for narrow sub-tasks only.

scenes.title: The directed cyclic graph
scenes.caption: States are nodes; transitions are edges. Cycles are the typical shape — a room that loops to itself, a proposal lifecycle that bounces draft↔review, a phase pipeline that retries on failure.
scenes.narration: States are nodes; transitions are edges. Cycles are the typical shape — retries, loops, back-arcs.

scenes.body: Every room is a state; not every state is a room. A room is a top-level state — the unit the operator lands on.
scenes.lede: Three hooks define a room's lifecycle: on_enter fires on every entry, on: maps intents to transitions, and effects on a transition edge run when that edge is taken. There is deliberately no on_exit — exit side effects belong on the transition leaving the room, so they're visible on the edge that causes them.
scenes.narration: Every room is a state; not every state is a room. Three hooks: on_enter, on colon, transition effects.

scenes.title: Room lifecycle
scenes.caption: on_enter re-fires on every entry — reload, self-re-entry, on_error redirect. Make it idempotent: prefer get-or-create host verbs and guard invokes on their own absence.
scenes.narration: On-enter fires effects. View renders. Turn arrives. Intent routes. Guards evaluate. Effects run. Next room entered.

scenes.body: A turn: the user said something; the story responds. Serialised through a per-session writer lock.
scenes.lede: Free text descends four routing tiers — deterministic match, synonym, slot template, turn cache — stopping at the first hit. The LLM is only called when all four miss. Then machine.Turn validates the intent, picks the first guarded transition, collects effects. Same world + same intent → same transition, always.
scenes.narration: Four routing tiers before the LLM. Same world plus same intent equals same transition, always.

scenes.title: Four routing tiers

turns.user: refine — add a negative JWT test case
turns.intent: refine {feedback: add a negative JWT test case}

scenes.caption: Tiers: deterministic (exact menu string or declared example), synonym (stem-bag match, ~3 µs), slot template ({slot}-capturing, <100 µs), turn cache (SQLite, ~80 µs), then LLM. Cache writes the LLM result so the same phrase is free next time.
scenes.narration: First turn falls through all tiers to the LLM. Second hits a synonym in microseconds. Third matches a slot template and extracts the feedback.

scenes.title: Intents and slots
scenes.caption: An intent is a named action — the atom of free-text translation. Slot validation runs before any guard, so a malformed payload can't slip past declared constraints. required:true forces the router through to the LLM when a value is absent.
scenes.narration: An intent is the atom of free-text translation. Slot validation runs before any guard.

scenes.title: Effects
scenes.caption: Effects are the enumerated alphabet of mutations — the LLM never writes the world directly, only effects the author declared do. They run in declaration order over an immutable per-turn world snapshot.
scenes.narration: Effects are the enumerated alphabet of mutations. The LLM never writes the world — only declared effects do.

scenes.body: Transitions fire when their guard expression is true. First match wins.
scenes.lede: Guards are pure boolean expressions over world state and slot values. At decision time the runtime evaluates each transition in declaration order and takes the first one whose guard passes. A transition with no guard (or default:true) is the catch-all.
scenes.narration: Guards are boolean expressions over world and slots. First match wins.

scenes.body: Traditional agents re-ingest the full conversation every turn. Kitsoki doesn't.
scenes.lede: Unstructured agents accumulate context quadratically — every new turn pays to re-process every prior turn, and the model choice is typically whatever the framework defaults to. Kitsoki's scoped world holds only what the story author declared relevant: the LLM sees a deterministically assembled context, never the raw session history. Model selection is explicit per step in YAML — local or cheap tiers for routing and simple decisions, expensive models only where the task justifies it. Scripts execute deterministically instead of asking the LLM to reason; semantic search answers lookups at near-zero cost; the turn cache makes repeated phrases free.
scenes.narration: Traditional agents re-ingest everything, quadratically. Kitsoki assembles context precisely: scoped world, model per step, scripts over reasoning, search over LLM.

scenes.title: Five cost levers

nodes.label: turn cache
lines: LLM called once per novel phrase

scenes.caption: Each lever is independent. Scoped world context and turn caching are structural — on by default. Model-per-step, semantic search, and script execution are explicit story-author choices that compound: a step that uses semantic search and a cheap local model for routing costs orders of magnitude less than a general-purpose agent call.
scenes.narration: Five independent levers. Scoped world and turn cache by design. Model per step, semantic search, and scripts are author choices that compound.

scenes.title: Bugfix story — 7 rooms
scenes.caption: A compute-room chain: on_enter binds a structured artifact into world, a gate advances the pipeline. The same graph runs with a human decider, an LLM judge (agent.decide), or fully autonomous — by setting execution mode, not rewriting the graph.
scenes.narration: A compute-room chain: on_enter produces an artifact, a gate advances. Same graph, different execution mode — human, LLM judge, or autonomous.

scenes.title: Bugfix: judge polymorphism
scenes.caption: The gate decider generalises next(). The same graph runs with a human decider, an agent.decide judge, or deterministically — the graph never forks, only the decider swaps. A single when: guard on emit_intent covers all three modes.
scenes.narration: The gate decider generalises next. Same graph, three modes — human, LLM judge, or deterministic.

scenes.title: PRD story — 5 rooms

nodes.label: idle
lines: discovery — one question at a time

scenes.caption: The PRD story contrasts with bugfix: idle is a live chat room, brief is deterministic (no agent), and clarify from drafting closes a multi-round clarification loop.
scenes.narration: PRD story: idle is a live chat. Brief is deterministic. Clarify from drafting closes a multi-round loop.

scenes.title: Host interfaces
scenes.caption: Stories call provider-neutral interfaces. The binding to a concrete handler (Jira, GitHub, local files) is set at story-load time — the same YAML runs in all environments.
scenes.narration: Stories call provider-neutral interfaces. The binding to Jira, GitHub, or local files is resolved at load time.

scenes.title: Definition schema — room
scenes.caption: view renders after on_enter bind settles — {{ world.artifact.field }} is already populated when the view runs. No pending fallbacks needed unless the bind is conditional. on: maps the declared intent alphabet to guarded transitions.
scenes.narration: View renders after on_enter bind settles — world is already populated. On colon maps declared intents to guarded transitions.
